title: AI-Generated Content
essence: Anything Nexus or a Claude skill produces — daily briefs, LinkedIn posts and carousels, Marp slide decks, outreach emails, vault notes — renders here. The atmosphere is editorial, not corporate. Considered, not breathless. The voice is the Expert Mate written down. The format borrows from print: clear hierarchy, tables when tables help, pull quotes when a customer's words deserve weight, white space when an idea needs to land. If a brief looks like ChatGPT default voice, it is wrong. If it looks like a half-page from a trade publication someone might actually want to read, it is right.
audience: Mixed. Internal readers of daily briefs. External readers of LinkedIn posts. Prospects reading proposals and outreach. The team reading session digests.

Brand.name: Brand Orange
Brand.role: Section anchors, pull-quote rules. One accent per page.

Brand.name: Print Orange
Brand.role: CMYK-safe orange for printed PDFs. CMYK won't reproduce #f05323 cleanly.

Editorial Light.name: Highlight
Editorial Light.role: Editorial highlight on a key claim — sparingly.

Editorial Dark.name: Page (dark)
Editorial Dark.role: LinkedIn dark background, dark slide decks.

typeScale.label: c-h2
typeScale.sample: What we are watching this week

typeScale.label: c-h3
typeScale.sample: AMC supply window slipping in WA

typeScale.label: c-lead
typeScale.sample: Crews in the Pilbara are asking why Carry-Flow stocks have moved without notice — here is what we know.

typeScale.label: c-body
typeScale.sample: Three weeks ago a Pilbara crew called Margaret because their HDD bore was losing returns. We had Loss-Swell on site within 48 hours and the bore was back in production by Wednesday. Specifics like this are why customers stay.

typeScale.label: c-pull
typeScale.sample: We are in the field with you, not just behind a desk.

fonts.use: Headings, captions, pull quotes, slide titles.
fonts.why: Sans heading paired against serif body is the editorial signal — it reads as authored, not auto-generated. The contrast is the entire point of this surface.
Serif body is what stops Nexus output reading like ChatGPT defaults. It is the most important font choice on this surface.

fonts.use: IDs, batch numbers, dates, technical detail in briefs.
fonts.why: Numbers and codes need to feel separable from prose — that is what makes a brief readable, not just legible.

spacing.label: x16
spacing.use: Page top padding for briefs and decks.

radii.label: sm
radii.use: Inline code chip — that is it.

shadows.label: none
shadows.use: Editorial is flat. White space is the only depth.

do: Lead every brief with a standfirst.
Source every claim — wikilink for vault, URL for external.
Use serif for body, sans for headings. Always.
One pull quote per long-form piece, max.
dont: Don't use emoji in published content. None.
Don't write 'Hope this finds you well', 'Just circling back', 'Curious to hear your thoughts'.
Don't use AI-flavoured imagery — glowing brains, neural lattices, hexagons.
Don't fabricate quotes, numbers, or sources. Hard rule.
Don't use em-dashes more than once per paragraph — readers notice when AI overdoes them.
Don't ship two CTAs in one email.
